series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, photographs and multimedia files in any and all formats, negatives, slides, and other output documenting events, activities, and other occasions for the Office of the Governor, or for the Development Services Agency. NOTE: Photographs and multimedia files that serve a temporary purpose and are of transient and limited value should be managed in accordance with the Agency’s Transient Documents retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format. If also stored in electronic/digital format, or if converted to electronic/digital format, paper records may be destroyed after verification of successful conversion and if no longer of value in paper form. ; Electronic: Retain in office or offsite storage during current administration LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Records in this series originate or reside in the Director''s office and include, but are not limited to, executive correspondence, memos, information, reports, and directives concerning agency operations and administration, policies, procedures, organization, and reviews, along with related records. NOTE: This series applies to records that document substantive actions, functions, policies, or procedures of the agency and may possess enduring value such that they require review by the State Archives after the required retention period has elapsed. Records that do not possess this quality may be managed with an appropriate alternative schedule such as DEV-GEN-01, DEV-GEN-02, or DEV-GEN-03 as applicable.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format. If converted to electronic format, paper files may ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, financial statements, budget reports, planning materials and supporting documentation, financial transaction records to include vouchers, requisitions, purchase orders, invoices, receipts, property inventories, collection records, accounting documents, and related records. This schedule is for NON-FEDERAL awards. Records directly pertaining to FEDERAL AWARDS are managed in accordance with Record Series "DEV-SVC-07: Federal Awards."
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes records that have short-lived administrative, legal or fiscal value. They convey information of temporary importance and are of no value once read/viewed/heard and/or acted upon. This series includes, but is not limited to: transient messages, notes, working drafts and other documents, regardless of media format, that serve to convey information of temporary value to which no lasting significance attaches.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of value LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed. ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of value LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes routine internal correspondence (emails, letters, memos), as well as correspondence from and responses to various individuals, companies, and organizations requesting information pertaining to agency and legal interpretations and other miscellaneous inquiries. General correspondence is informative (it does not attempt to influence agency policy.)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain six (6) months LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes those records pertaining to ancillary or non-substantial matters, activities and tasks that are handled solely in accordance with existing policies but which, due to their varied or irregular nature, do not accurately correlate with the description set forth in any other series.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain one (1) year after close of matter, activity, or task, or until superseded or obsolete LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to: hiring documents such as employee applications, resumes, interview notes, position descriptions, employment agreements, transcripts, certificates, letters of recommendation and other documents relative to the position; personnel actions; tax forms; medical or personal information; criminal records; employee FMLA, EAP, disability, and workers compensation; letters of resignation; any other record maintained as part of the official employee personnel file. NOTE: The Ohio Department of Administrative Services (DAS) is responsible for archiving and permanently maintaining employee files for the state. DAS routinely receives the required documents through the procedures that enact personnel actions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain seven (7) years after employee leaves employment EXCEPTION TO RETENTION PERIOD: As set forth in the collective bargaining agreement and the Ohio Administrative Code, certain documents must be removed from the official employee personnel file after a specified length of time. Refer to the current collective bargaining agreement and the applicable OAC provisions. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to: hiring materials and posting files for unsuccessful applicants; contingent workforce records; workforce development; health, safety and incident records; internal investigations; civil rights and Title VII records; requests for outside agency to investigate; employee training records; tuition reimbursement and other education assistance; discipline and grievance records; other operational records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years from the record date or the date of final disposition of an action (if applicable) NOTE: In the event that the collective bargaining agreement specifies retention and record storage obligations that differ from the obligations set forth in this schedule, the terms in collective bargaining agreement shall take precedence over this schedule. Refer to the terms of current agreement and the applicable OAC provisions. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to: organizational charts; training manuals; position descriptions; unsolicited resumes and related documents; other administrative documents which have no value once superseded or obsolete.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain six (6) months or until superseded or obsolete LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_title: PUBLIC RECORDS AND OTHER THIRD-PARTY REQUESTS
series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, original request, related correspondence, research, response, and other records relating to public records requests or other third-party requests upon the agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years from date of final reply or resolution LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_title: CLAIMS, LITIGATION, COLLECTION AND INVESTIGATION FILES
series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, documents relative to legal matters of which the Ohio Development Services Agency is, or may be, a party. Also includes certifications, administrative hearings, internal or external investigations, correspondence, legal opinions, and other records related to actual or potential legal matters.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office area for two (2) years after case is closed and no further appeals can be filed LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed. ; Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format

series_description: All records pertaining to grants including, but not limited to, applications, grant agreements, amendments, supporting documentation, exhibits, disbursements, reports, royalty agreements, correspondence, award memos, and other records filed with and related to the grant.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three (3) years after the end of post-term reporting, or for unfunded/unsuccessful applications retain two (2) years after date of denial Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed. ; Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format

series_description: All records pertaining to the tax incentive agreement including, but not limited to, applications, agreements, amendments, supporting documentation, reports, correspondence, and other records filed with and related to the tax incentive agreement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: AGREEMENTS NOT SUBJECT TO CLAWBACK: Retain three (3) years after closeout or post-term reporting (if required) AGREEMENTS SUBJECT TO CLAWBACK: Retain for the later of [a] three (3) years after the end of post-term reporting, or [b] two (2) years after the expiration of the clawback period; then destroy. UNFUNDED/UNSUCCESSFUL APPLICATIONS: retain two (2) years after date of denial, then destroy. Statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect retention of related records. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: All records pertaining to contracts including, but not limited to, RFPs, evaluations, contracts for goods or services, amendments and modifications, supporting documentation, reports, correspondence, and other records filed with and related to the contract.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five (5) years after expiration date or termination of contract, or for unsuccessful proposals/bids retain two (2) years after contract award date ; Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format

series_description: All records pertaining to the issuance of bonds, including, but not limited to, applications, financial statements, reports, employee reports, annual reports, legal transcripts, notice of intent, notice of issuance, correspondence, ongoing audits and other records filed with and related to the bond issue.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain three (3) years after maturity or satisfaction of all obligations Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes all records pertaining to the issuance of surety bonds including, but not limited to, pre-qualification records, funding verification, financial statements, bond issue documents and all other records filed with and related to the surety bonds.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain six (6) years after completion of project or such shorter limitations to bring claims if contractually agreed upon. For unsuccessful/unfunded applications and unexecuted projects: retain one (1) year after date of last action. Statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect retention of related records. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed. ; Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format

series_description: All records which document the expenditure & use of federal monies awarded to state agencies.
retention_statement: Unknown: Uniform record retention requirements for federal awards, as determined by the U.S. Office of Management and Budget, are set forth in the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations at 2 CFR 200.334 and are generally three years from a specified date. Refer to 2 CFR 200.334 to determine the commencement of the 3-year period for each type of record. Some federal programs mandate retention requirements specific to the program. Statutory and regulatory provisions applicable to specific programs take precedence over the uniform record retention requirements. ; Electronic: HOME INVESTMENT PARTNERSHIP (HOME): retention governed by 24 CFR 91.508(c). Records for each FY must be retained for the most recent 5-year period, except homeownership project records must be retained 5 years after project completion date & written agreements must be retained 5 years after agreement ends. For commencement of the 5-year retention period for rental housing projects, tenant-based rental assistance projects, & displacements and acquisition see 24 CFR 91.508(c). If litigation, claim, negotiation, audit, monitoring, inspection or other action has commenced before record retention period ends records must be retained for the later of completion of the action & resolution of issues or the end of required period. ALL OTHER PROGRAMS: Retain 3 years from date of submission of final expenditure report or, for awards renewed quarterly or annually, from date of submission of quarterly/annual financial report as reported to the Federal awarding agency or pass-through entity. ; Paper: Retain for the period specified for electronic format
disposition: Unknown: Other ; Electronic: Erase ; Paper: Shred

series_description: Includes application materials and supporting documentation for individual applications, including, but not limited to, eligibility information, inspections, payments, denials, appeals, and other documents filed with and related to the applicant.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: FEDERAL PROGRAMS: Retain three (3) years after closeout of file, or three (3) years after date of denial for unfunded/unsuccessful applications NON-FEDERAL PROGRAMS: Retain two (2) years after closeout of file, or (2) years after date of denial for unfunded/unsuccessful applications, then destroy. Statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect retention of related records. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, notes, reports, guidance, correspondence, and other documents filed with and related to the administration of the program or project.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years after completion of program or project, or until superseded or obsolete. (Federal or state statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect record retention periods. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule.) Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. Upon expiration of the retention period: STATE ARCHIVE REVIEW IS REQUIRED for state plans and official annual and final reports. ALL OTHER RECORDS IN THIS SERIES: destroy after the retention period has elapsed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_title: BOARD, COMMISSIONS, COUNCILS AND AUTHORITIES MEETING FILES
series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, meeting subject files, applications, supporting documentation, staff write up and reviews, presentation materials and resolutions, minutes, and other related documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain for the term of project (if applicable) plus two years. AFTER EXPIRATION OF RETENTION PERIOD: STATE ARCHIVE REVIEW IS REQUIRED for the following: official meeting minutes, formation and establishment records, bylaws, resolutions, official reports, appointment and designee letters, and reports to the General Assembly.. ALL OTHER RECORDS IN THIS SERIES: destroy after the retention period has elapsed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, records created for Tourism and Travel campaigns, publications, surveys, marketing and advertising programs, records that document Ohio''s heritage, art, culture, activities, and destinations, reports and related files and information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain in office area for four (4) years NOTE: This series applies to records that may possess enduring value and therefore require review by the State Archives after the required retention period has elapsed. Records that do not possess this quality may be managed with an appropriate alternative schedule such as DEV-GEN-01, DEV-GEN-02, or DEV-GEN-03 as applicable. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, news/press releases and bulletins, media advisories, agency updates and announcements, media contacts, e-mail distribution lists, e-mail confirmations, return notices, statistical data, address information, supporting documentation, and other related records. NOTE: This series applies to records created or received by the Communications Office that may possess enduring value and therefore require review by the State Archives after the required retention period has elapsed. Records that do not possess this quality may be managed with an appropriate alternative schedule such as DEV-GEN-01, DEV-GEN-02, or DEV-GEN-03 as applicable.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_title: LOCAL TAX INCENTIVE AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENTS AND CERTIFICATIONS
series_description: Records related to tax incentive and economic development agreements administered by municipal and county governments, including, but not limited to, petitions and supporting documentation, agreements, amendments, confirmation certificate or letter of denial, annual reports, and other records related to and filed with the tax exemption authority certifications and agreements. The Agency is not a party to these agreements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: CERTIFICATION DOCUMENTS AND AGREEMENTS: Retain for the duration of the agreement or certification plus two years ANNUAL REPORTS: Retain for five (5) years. Annual reports compiled by the Agency must then be reviewed by the State prior to disposal. SUPPLEMENTAL ITEMS: Retain for five (5) years The Agency is not a party to the agreements and supplemental items do not serve to document the agency''s role. Certification of an area or program is based upon whether it has the characteristics set forth in the applicable section of the Ohio Revised Code. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_title: AUDIT FILES AND SINGLE AUDIT REPORTS
series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, audit reports completed by either the Auditor of State or private accounting firms, with supporting papers from our review of the reports, related correspondence, sub-grantee application, grant contract and amendments, budget data, list of reimbursements by state, contracts entered into by grantee, financial report from grantee, audit information, indirect cost plan and rate, board minutes, and other documents related to and filed with the record. This schedule satisfies the minimum required retention period set forth in the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations at 2 CFR §200.517
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for two (2) years ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) years LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: All records pertaining to loans or collateral enhancement agreements, including, but not limited to, applications, agreements, amendments, supporting documentation, disbursements, reports, correspondence, invoices, financial statements, and other records filed with and related to the agreement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format ; Electronic: Retain three (3) years after maturity or satisfaction of all obligations, whichever is later, or for unfunded/unsuccessful applications retain two (2) years after date of denial Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. NOTE: Quarterly financial statements may be destroyed when superseded by annual statements. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: This record series pertains ONLY to research and development projects financed in whole or in part with the proceeds of a Third Frontier bond issue. Records include, but are not limited to, applications, grant agreements and ancillary agreements, amendments, supporting documentation, exhibits, award letters, invoices, payments, reports, correspondence, and other records filed with and related to the project.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain hard copies of the original Project file for at least six (6) years after the effective date of the Project. ; Electronic: Retain "Project Data" during the three-year period following the final maturity or redemption date of the bond issue from which funds were used to support the Project, or, if later, while any refunding bonds that refund the bond issue are outstanding and for the three-year period following the final maturity or redemption date of any of the refunding bonds. "Project Data" will include: the name of the grant recipient, a brief project description, a summary of the scope of work, and vendor payment information. NOTES: Statutory or regulatory provisions may affect retention requirements and will take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Records subject to litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.

series_description: All records pertaining to programs to ensure equal opportunity in government contracting, including business certification programs such as Minority Business Enterprise (MBE), Encouraging Diversity, Growth, and Equity (EDGE), Women-Owned Business Enterprise (WBE), Veteran-Friendly Business Enterprise (VBE) and other disadvantaged business certification programs, as well as state agency contractor utilization reporting, and Construction Compliance. Documents in this series include, but are not limited to: initial, cross-certification and recertification applications or affidavits and their supporting documentation; certificates; compliance monitoring records; all applicable reports; notifications; affirmative action plans; complaints; denials; revocations; appeals; records of hearings; adjudication matters; correspondence; annual procurement percentage goals for each agency; annual expenditure projection reports and quarterly expenditure reports filed by each agency; and all other records filed with and related to programs and initiatives to ensure equal opportunity in government contracting. NOTES: Statutory or regulatory provisions applicable to certain programs may affect retention of related records. In such cases, the statutory or regulatory provisions shall take precedence over the retention language set forth in this schedule. Records subject to audit shall be further retained until audit report is released and discrepancies addressed. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain three years after business’s participation in program ends, or all rights to appeal have been exhausted, whichever is later. If converted to electronic/digital format, paper records may be destroyed after verification of successful conversion. ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain three years after business’s participation in program ends, or all rights to appeal have been exhausted, whichever is later.
